mechanism_narrative: Declining federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing equity valuations. A 1-month decline in FEDFUNDS predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days through this valuation channel.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 55% of instances where FEDFUNDS declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals strengthening labor demand and consumer income growth, which drives corporate earnings expectations upward and produces positive equity returns over 60 trading days. The mechanism operates through improved earnings visibility and reduced recession risk.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns fail to exceed baseline by a statistically significant margin (t-test p > 0.10) when PAYEMS growth is positive.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) contraction signals weakening consumer demand and foreshadows earnings downgrades. When RSAFS declines by more than 2% month-over-month, S&P 500 forward returns turn negative over the next 20 trading days as equity prices reprice lower earnings expectations.
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.06 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger is active.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth reflects real economic capacity utilization and pricing power. When INDPRO accelerates above its 3-month trend by more than 0.5 index points, S&P 500 returns are positive over 5 trading days due to improved profit margins and reduced stagflation risk.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO acceleration > 0.5 index points above 3-month trend
failure_condition: Positive 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.08 over any 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising unemployment (UNRATE) combined with declining payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals labor market deterioration that triggers equity repricing. The joint deterioration in both series predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days as recession risk increases and earnings forecasts are cut.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp AND PAYEMS declines > 100k in 1 month
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 65% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect (joint signal) shows no statistically significant incremental predictive power over single-series signals (F-test p > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly measure consumer demand momentum. When both series show month-over-month growth above their 6-month median, S&P 500 forward returns are positive over 60 trading days due to sustained consumption and investment demand driving earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: HOUST and RSAFS both grow > 6-month median growth rate
failure_condition: Positive 60-day forward returns occur in fewer than 62% of triggered instances, or the interaction term contributes no significant explanatory power beyond additive single-series effects (R² improvement < 0.02).

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) above 3% combined with elevated federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS > 2.5%) signals monetary tightening regime. In this regime, S&P 500 forward returns are negative over 20 trading days as real discount rates remain high and growth expectations are suppressed.
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Negative 20-day forward returns occur in fewer than 58% of triggered instances, or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 2-year rolling window when trigger conditions are met.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) decline combined with rising unemployment (UNRATE) signals stagflationary pressure or demand destruction. This joint deterioration predicts negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days as equity investors reprice earnings downside and increase risk premiums.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines > 0.3 index points AND UNRATE rises > 0.2pp
failure_condition: Negative 5-day forward returns occur in fewer than 60% of triggered instances, or the joint signal shows no incremental predictive power over single-series signals (Vuong test p > 0.20).
